fields.name: capabilities
documentation: Defines the capabilities provided by the client.
Capabilities provided by the client (editor or tool), at startup.

fields.name: commands
documentation: (field) vim.lsp.Client.commands: table<string, fun(command: lsp.Command, ctx: table)>

fields.name: config
documentation: Copy of the config passed to |vim.lsp.start()|.

fields.name: flags
documentation: A table with flags for the client. The current (experimental) flags are:

fields.name: handlers
documentation: (field) vim.lsp.Client.handlers: table<string, fun(err?: lsp.ResponseError, result: any, context: lsp.HandlerContext, config?: table):...unknown>

fields.name: id
documentation: The id allocated to the client.

fields.name: progress
documentation: A ring buffer (|vim.ringbuf()|) containing progress messages

fields.name: requests
documentation: (field) vim.lsp.Client.requests: table<integer, { type: string, bufnr: integer, method: string }?>
The current pending requests in flight to the server. Entries are key-value
pairs with the key being the request id while the value is a table with
for an active request, or "cancel" for a cancel request. It will be
"complete" ephemerally while executing |LspRequest| autocmds when replies

fields.name: rpc
documentation: RPC client object, for low level interaction with the client.

fields.name: server_capabilities
documentation: Defines the capabilities provided by a language
Response from the server sent on `initialize` describing the server's capabilities.

fields.name: server_info
documentation: Response from the server sent on `initialize` describing server information (e.g. version).

fields.name: _add_workspace_folder
documentation: Add a directory to the workspace folders.

fields.name: _get_registration_options
documentation: Get options for a method that is registered dynamically.

fields.name: _remove_workspace_folder
documentation: Remove a directory to the workspace folders.

fields.name: _supports_registration
documentation: Get options for a method that is registered dynamically.

fields.name: _text_document_did_open_handler
documentation: Default handler for the 'textDocument/didOpen' LSP notification.
@*param* `bufnr` — Number of the buffer, or 0 for current

fields.name: cancel_request
documentation: Cancels a request with a given request id.
@*return* `status` — indicating if the notification was successful.

fields.name: exec_cmd
documentation: (method) vim.lsp.Client:exec_cmd(command: lsp.Command, context?: { bufnr: integer }, handler?: fun(err?: lsp.ResponseError, result: any, context: lsp.HandlerContext, config?: table):...unknown)
Execute a lsp command, either via client command function (if available)
or via workspace/executeCommand (if supported by the server)
@*param* `handler` — only called if a server command

fields.name: is_stopped
documentation: Checks whether a client is stopped.
@*return* — true if client is stopped or in the process of being

fields.name: notify
documentation: Sends a notification to an LSP server.
@*return* `status` — indicating if the notification was successful.
If it is false, then the client has shutdown.

fields.name: on_attach
documentation: Runs the on_attach function from the client's config if it was defined.

fields.name: request
documentation: (method) vim.lsp.Client:request(method: string, params?: table, handler?: fun(err?: lsp.ResponseError, result: any, context: lsp.HandlerContext, config?: table):...unknown, bufnr?: integer)
Sends a request to the server.
This is a thin wrapper around {client.rpc.request} with some additional
checks for capabilities and handler availability.
@*return* `status` — indicates whether the request was successful.
If it is `false`, then it will always be `false` (the client has shutdown).

fields.name: request_sync
documentation: (method) vim.lsp.Client:request_sync(method: string, params: table, timeout_ms?: integer, bufnr?: integer)
Sends a request to the server and synchronously waits for the response.
@*param* `timeout_ms` — Maximum time in milliseconds to wait for
@*return* `err` — On timeout, cancel or error, where `err` is a

fields.name: stop
documentation: Stops a client, optionally with force.
By default, it will just request the server to shutdown without force. If
you request to stop a client which has previously been requested to
shutdown, it will automatically escalate and force shutdown.

fields.name: supports_method
documentation: Checks if a client supports a given method.
Always returns true for unknown off-spec methods.
Note: Some language server capabilities can be file specific.
